_comment: LLM-MAINTAINED MODULE MAP for @argo/desktop. Single source of truth for module boundaries; .dependency-cruiser.cjs turns it into lint rules mechanically — edit this map, never the generated rules. Refresh when the src/ structure changes. Managed by the setup-module-boundaries skill.

modules.name: shared
modules.comment: Cross-process contract: the Session state union, the ship facts main observes, the event vocabulary, projected-state types, IPC channel names, and the pure projection reducer. Types-and-pure-functions only — imported by all three processes (renderer files reach it through the `@shared` alias), imports none of them.

modules.name: main
modules.comment: Electron main process. Its internals are private; the app is wired only from its own entry.

modules.name: preload
modules.comment: The context-bridge. Loaded by Electron via file path, not imported — nothing should import its internals.

modules.name: renderer
modules.comment: The UI (browser context). Talks to main only through the window bridge, never by import.

modules.name: ship
modules.comment: Pure ship-flow derivation inside the renderer: the contract's session facts → ribbon nodes, head, terminal, and the rail status word (cockpit-matrix.md). No React, no I/O — it declares no facts of its own, and Views consume the derived model through its barrel and never re-derive it.

layers._comment: Electron process isolation: the three processes must never import each other at all — the only channels are the preload bridge (renderer↔main) and Electron's own preload wiring (main→preload by path). Importing across them is the classic footgun this locks out. All three may import `shared` (the type/pure-function contract); `shared` must import none of them.
